Methodology Track Record Case Studies Library About Engage

A strategic consultancy · Practiced as decision cartography

We map the path. We defend the map.

Power In Numbers is a strategic consultancy. Our discipline is decision cartography — a proprietary methodology, validated by VERIDEX, with thirty-two years of pattern recognition, six firm-built ventures, and applications at sovereign fiscal scale. The methodology has been proven on us first.

32 years of methodology · 6 firm-built ventures · $30B sovereign framework mapped · 22/22 VERIDEX gates passing
See the track record Read the methodology
§ 01 The architecture

A discipline, not a deck of services.

Power In Numbers is built as a stack. Worldview underwrites methodology; methodology produces outputs; validation governs the whole. The brand is the connective tissue.

§ 02 Three pillars of proof

The methodology has been proven on us first.

Most strategy firms validate their thinking with client wins. We validate ours by predicting markets before they exist, by building ventures we ourselves operate, and by submitting our outputs to VERIDEX gates at sovereign scale. Three independent proofs, each on its own page.

Pillar 01 Predictive Track Record

The methodology has identified category-defining patterns five to fifteen years before each became a market trend.

For: Press · academics · journalists · conference programmers

Six documented instances across thirty-two years.

See the timeline
Pillar 02 Firm-Built Proof

The major strategy consultancies build ventures for their clients. Power In Numbers builds with itself.

For: Founders · operators · enterprise buyers · venture investors

Six firm-owned operating ventures.

See the ventures
Pillar 03 Sovereign-Scale Proof

The methodology produces VERIDEX-validated outputs at the scale of national fiscal frameworks.

For: Ministries · multilaterals · sovereign wealth funds

$30B Ghana fiscal framework · 22/22 VERIDEX gates.

See the Ghana case
§ 03 Predictive track record

Six patterns, each documented before the market named it.

A timeline of category-defining moves implemented by Power In Numbers five to fifteen years before they became mainstream — with primary-source evidence on each entry.

  1. 2017-18 The Foundation (El Paso, TX flagship). 100% community-owned venue: 50% artist-celebrity collective + 50% public via lifetime seat-ownership rights with per-event resale. V.I.P. member-controlled access. Serialized fractional tokens (EP-FLAG-999, 0001 Foundation Coin). Operated through 9th Ward Production and Promotions Company, LLC, with the project funded through S4TF, LTD. vs. Venu Holding Corporation (NYSE: VENU) ≈7 years yrs ahead
  2. 2010-11 Go Local Movement launched in Houston, Texas, with Go Local Magazine — a periodical built CROWD POWERED. Local entrepreneurs were the contributors, advertisers, and editorial subjects. Edition 1 (February 2011) names Power In Numbers explicitly and traces its origin from Mary Leamons's Go Local Advertising company through Khalif Ibere and Kwa Ko-A-Lah to Jay Davis. vs. The International Telegraph (and the broader hyperlocal-media category) ≈15 years yrs ahead
  3. 2009 First mystery / surprise gift-box subscription using liquidator products at below-wholesale prices, with brands using the service as a marketing channel. Pilot in Thomaston, GA, expanding through Atlanta-metro. vs. Birchbox 1 year (Birchbox); 2–5 years (broader mystery-box category) yrs ahead
  4. 2008 VerSes head-to-head artist battle format at Z88-WBKZ (Athens, GA), operating publicly with independent artists. Same format mirrored later as Verzuz. vs. Verzuz 12 years yrs ahead
See the full timeline
§ 04 From the library

The publishing engine.

White papers, frameworks, track-record essays, and case studies. Citation-grade where the audience is academic; press-grade where the moment demands it.

  • Firm-built essay

    Ghana — Reading the AI Mirror Engine Output

    The AI Mirror Stage-3 Full Report on Dr. Janelle Thompson's Ghana market entry is the engine's published reference deliverable. This essay is the reader's guide to the two pathways, why Pathway B was recommended, and what the cartography is doing that the summary numbers cannot do alone.

    April 25, 2026
  • Firm-built essay

    The Master's Chair — Reading the Engine Output

    The VERIDEX BI — USA Stage-3 Full Report on The Master's Chair's Atlanta expansion is the engine's published reference deliverable. The principal has accepted Pathway A. Implementation has not yet begun. This essay is the reader's guide to the four numbers a principal is most likely to ask about — and what each is doing in the analysis.

    April 25, 2026
  • Methodology note

    Sourcing — The First Family of the Twenty-Two Gates

    The Sourcing family is five gates — G·01, G·02, G·03, G·19, G·20 — and it governs every numeric claim in a VERIDEX-validated deliverable. This note walks through the family at the gate level, with the bank-letter sourcing in the Foundation Project dossier as the worked example.

    April 25, 2026
Browse the library
§ 05 Engage

Three ways to put the methodology to work.

We work selectively. Each tier has a separate intake. State the decision on the table and the stakes; we will respond with whether and how we can help.

Tier 01 · Sovereign & institutional

Request a sovereign briefing.

Ministries, multilaterals, and sovereign wealth funds. Fiscal frameworks, policy architecture, and counterfactual landscapes at $30B-plus scale.

Open the sovereign intake →
Tier 02 · Enterprise

Map your decision.

For executive teams facing a decision with strategic counterfactuals, constituency dynamics, or category-creation stakes. Mapped, scored, defended.

Open the enterprise intake →
Tier 03 · Founder & venture

Apply for a venture cartography session.

For founders and operators with a venture in motion and a hard question on the table. Limited slate; selection is by fit, not first-come.

Apply for a session →
